data.title: Investar Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87, revenue of $35.64M
data.description:
• Investar press release (ISTR): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87.
  • Revenue of $35.64M.

  • Net interest margin improved 39 basis points to 3.59% for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to 3.20% for the quarter ended December 31, 2025. Exclusive of the interest income accretion from the acquisition of loans and interest recoveries, adjusted net interest margin improved eight basis points to 3.28% for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to 3.20% for the quarter ended December 31, 2025.

  • Diluted earnings per common share were $0.77 for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to $0.51 for the quarter ended December 31, 2025. Core diluted earnings per common share were $0.87 for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to $0.58 for the quarter ended December 31, 2025.

  • Return on average assets increased to 1.25% for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to 0.83% for the quarter ended December 31, 2025. Core return on average assets improved to 1.41% for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to 0.93% for the quarter ended December 31, 2025.

  • Efficiency ratio improved to 64.08% for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to 69.34% for the quarter ended December 31, 2025. Core efficiency ratio improved to 58.46% for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to 66.13% for the quarter ended December 31, 2025.

  • The yield on the loan portfolio increased to 6.28% for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to 5.99% for the quarter ended December 31, 2025.

  • The overall cost of funds for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 decreased four basis points to 2.94% compared to 2.98% for the quarter ended December 31, 2025. The cost of deposits decreased six basis points to 2.85% for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 compared to 2.91% for the quarter ended December 31, 2025.

  • Total loans increased by $891.8 million, or 41.0%, to $3.07 billion at March 31, 2026 compared to $2.18 billion at December 31, 2025.

  • Variable-rate loans as a percentage of total loans was 49% at March 31, 2026 compared to 38% at December 31, 2025.

  • Book value per common share increased to $27.97 at March 31, 2026, or 1.2%, compared to $27.63 at December 31, 2025. Tangible book value per common share decreased to $22.72 at March 31, 2026, or 3.0%, compared to $23.42 at December 31, 2025, which represents minimal dilution related to our acquisition of WFB.

  • Total deposits increased by $882.6 million, or 37.6%, to $3.23 billion at March 31, 2026 compared to $2.35 billion at December 31, 2025.

  • Investar repurchased 53,420 shares of its common stock through its stock repurchase program at an average price of $28.63 per share during the quarter ended March 31, 2026, leaving 327,976 shares authorized for repurchase under the program at March 31, 2026.

  •

data.title: Bristow Group announces retirement of COO, Government Services Alan Corbett
data.description:
• Bristow Group (VTOL) on Monday announced that Alan Corbett, Chief Operating Officer, Government Services, has informed the company of his intention to retire at the end of this year.
  • Corbett will remain in his role through the appointment of a successor and will support a structured leadership transition.
  • Corbett joined Bristow in 2014 and has held several senior leadership positions across the company's international operations, including Region Director of the Europe Business Unit, Region Director of the Europe Caspian Region, Vice President of EAMEA, and Senior Vice President for Europe, Africa, Middle East, Asia, and Search and Rescue. He was appointed to his current role of Chief Operating Officer, Government Services, in February 2023.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Capital City Bank GAAP EPS of $0.92
data.description:
• Capital City Bank press release (CCBG): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.92.
  • Revenue of $62.15M
  • Tax-equivalent net interest income totaled $42.9 million compared to $43.4 million for the prior quarter and reflected two less calendar days in the first quarter
    • Net interest margin decreased two basis points to 4.24%
    • Credit quality metrics remained stable, with net loan charge‑offs of 10 basis points (annualized) of average loans, while the allowance coverage ratio increased one basis point to 1.23% as of March 31, 2026
    • Noninterest income decreased $0.2 million, or 0.8%, and reflected lower wealth management fees of $0.5 million and deposit fees of $0.2 million, partially offset by a miscellaneous recovery of $0.5 million
    • Noninterest expense decreased $1.5 million, or 3.5%, primarily due to a $2.7 million decrease in compensation expense (lower performance-based incentives) that was partially offset by an increase in other expense which reflected a $1.5 million pension plan settlement gain recognized in the prior quarter 

    Balance Sheet

    • Loan balances decreased $29.8 million, or 1.2% (average), and decreased $27.7 million, or 1.1% (end of period)
    • Deposit balances increased by $43.5 million, or 1.2% (average), and increased $89.3 million, or 2.4% (end of period), driven by strong core deposit growth
    • Tangible book value per diluted share (non-GAAP financial measure) increased $0.48, or 1.8%
    • Repurchased 63,088 shares of our common stock

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Monday: PEP, LLY, MRVL, ASTS
data.description:
Stock index futures were lower on Monday as optimism over a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal faded amid escalating tensions. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

Eli Lilly (LLY) slipped 0.19% in premarket trading amid reports the pharmaceutical giant is in advanced negotiations to acquire Kelonia Therapeutics in a transaction valued at more than $2 billion. According to The Wall Street Journal, the agreement could be announced as soon as Monday if talks are completed successfully. The final structure may also include milestone-based payments tied to Kelonia’s future progress.

Marvell Technology (MRVL) gained more than 6% before the opening bell after reports emerged that the specialty semiconductor company may work with Google on developing future artificial intelligence chips. The news weighed on existing Google partners, with Broadcom and Celestica both declining 1.5% and 1%, respectively, on the report.

PepsiCo (PEP) rose 0.21% in premarket trade after launching a major brand refresh for its Gatorade brand that shifts its identity from being purely a sports drink for athletes to a broader everyday hydration platform. The refresh includes new packaging, cleaner formulas, and products aimed at non-athlete occasions like work, travel, and daily routines. Under the company’s new Gatorade Advanced Hydration System, the lineup is organized by whether each drink hydrates better, faster, or longer than water.

AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) fell 12.7% in premarket trading after Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket failed to place a satellite built by the Texas-based company into its intended orbit. While the satellite separated from New Glenn’s second stage, it entered an “off-nominal orbit,” Blue Origin said. The launch marked Blue Origin’s third New Glenn mission and its first successful booster reuse.

data.title: Astra posts another late-stage trial win for COPD therapy
data.description:
AstraZeneca (AZN) announced on Monday another late-stage trial win for its antibody therapy, tozorakimab, which is targeted at patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, a respiratory disease characterized by airflow limitation and airway inflammation.

Citing data from its pivotal Phase 3 MIRANDA trial, the Anglo-Swedish drugmaker said that tozorakimab cut the annualized rate of moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations with a statistically significant and clinically meaningful effect.

Both the primary population of former smokers and the overall study population comprising former and current smokers experienced the effect regardless of the stage of their lung function severity and blood eosinophil counts.

The 52-week study was designed to evaluate tozorakimab 300 mg versus placebo in adults with symptomatic COPD as a treatment administered once every two weeks on top of standard of care inhaled therapy. The company plans to submit the results to global regulators and present them at a future medical event.

The interleukin-33-targeting antibody was generally well tolerated with a favorable safety profile in line with prior trials, AstraZeneca (AZN) added. The results build on positive data the company disclosed in March from its Phase 3 trials OBERON and TITANIA for tozorakimab in COPD.

data.title: Markets misreading Iran risk as rally signal despite escalation threat, BCA’s Gertken says- CNBC interview
data.description:
The financial markets are dangerously optimistic about the Iran-U.S. conflict, treating wartime disinformation as a reason to rally when investors should be preparing for prolonged uncertainty, Matt Gertken, chief geopolitical strategist at BCA Research, said. 

Gertken, in an interview with CNBC, cautioned that even if tensions de-escalate in the coming months, the unresolved nuclear issue at the heart of the conflict could trigger re-escalation later this year.

“Markets are finally starting to see this. The problem is that the market is reading it as a reason to get bullish because it’s saying, you know, we want to get back to the rally,” the strategist said. He emphasized that during the “fog of war, communications are deliberately deceptive” with disinformation coming from both sides.

Gertken outlined Iran’s apparent strategy of engaging President Trump in negotiations while simultaneously dragging their feet to keep oil prices uncomfortably high.

This approach aims to galvanize policy change in the United States by exploiting deep public divisions over the conflict. The tentative nature of upcoming talks—evidenced by Vice President JD Vance’s absence and Iran’s uncertain participation—reflects this strategy of making negotiations “very tenuous, very flimsy.”

While the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports is beginning to impact Iranian government revenue, the strategist noted that American consumers and the president himself have a “very low pain threshold” due to upcoming midterm elections.

With oil at $100 per barrel translating to roughly $4 per gallon of gasoline, Americans historically become dissatisfied—creating political vulnerability for the administration.

“The markets are wrong on both counts,” Gertken said, explaining that President Trump’s pain point is lower than investors assume, while Iran’s threshold is much higher.

Unlike the “liberation day” scenario where Trump can raise and lower tensions at will, Iran has been attacked, and “they have a higher payment threshold, and they’re not really integrated in global markets.”

Looking ahead, the strategist warned that the president faces a fundamental choice between pursuing long-term national security objectives—including nuclear nonproliferation and control of the Strait of Hormuz—and protecting Republican prospects in the midterm elections.

If the election “becomes a lost cause, the president’s incentives flip” toward more aggressive action, potentially including attacks on Iranian economic capacity or even regime change efforts.

data.title: Germany’s industrial slump fuels defense boom as carmakers pivot to arms
data.description:
Germany’s weakening auto-led export economy is driving a major shift toward defense manufacturing, creating new opportunities in aerospace, military suppliers and industrial firms tied to rearmament. For investors, the pivot signals a structural reallocation of capital toward European defense spending as governments boost budgets and seek greater security independence.

Germany is trying to turn industrial decline into a defense revival, shifting from cars to weapons as its long-dominant manufacturing model falters, The Wall Street Journal reported Monday.

The country is enduring its longest economic stagnation since World War II, pressured by weaker global demand, high costs and rising Chinese competition. Roughly 15,000 manufacturing jobs are disappearing each month, while major automakers have reported steep profit declines and job cuts. Mercedes-Benz (MBGAF) (MBGYY) (BENZ:CA) said 2025 profit fell 49%, while Volkswagen (VWAGY) reported a 44% drop and plans to eliminate 50,000 German jobs by 2030.

Berlin’s answer is to redirect industrial capacity toward defense production as Europe accelerates military spending and questions the durability of U.S. security support.

For investors, the pivot signals a structural reallocation of capital toward European defense spending as governments boost budgets and seek greater security independence.

Auto supplier Schaeffler (SFFLY) (SCAFF), which generates about €24 billion in annual revenue, now produces drone engines, armored vehicle systems and aviation components. Chief Executive Klaus Rosenfeld said the company wants defense to account for 10% of sales.

Across Germany, factories are being retooled for military output. Volkswagen (VWAGY) is in talks to make parts for Israel’s Iron Dome system by 2027, while Patriot interceptor missiles are expected to be produced in Germany to meet demand.

Economy Minister Katherina Reiche said Europe must build a defense industry it can rely on. Her ministry is backing efforts to connect struggling manufacturers with defense supply chains.

The shift is also attracting private capital. Government figures show nearly 90% of European venture funding for defense technology is flowing to German companies.

Engine maker Deutz (DEUZF), founded more than 160 years ago, has already entered the sector, supplying power-generation engines for Patriot systems and other military vehicles. The company said revenue rose 15% last year without large layoffs, as workers moved into defense production.

data.title: Electric vehicle sales soar in India amid concerns about emissions rules, gas prices
data.description:
Electric vehicle sales in India got a recent boost after concerns about new emission rules and higher fuel costs from the Iran conflict made more buyers consider non-gasoline options. Sales rose 82% year over year to 24,148 units during March, and full-year sales climbed 87% to 233,246, according to JATO Dynamics. 

While higher gas prices due to the Iran-Israel-U.S. conflict were a factor, the bigger concern for Indian car owners is upcoming emissions rules, which will force automakers to cut average fleet carbon emissions sharply starting next April and even more by 2032.

Despite the notable jump in sales this year, EVs still account for only about 5% of India's total car market, held back by a lack of affordable models, weak charging infrastructure, poor resale value, and range anxiety. EV sales in India remain smaller in terms of market share than those in Thailand, Vietnam, and Indonesia, even though they are growing quickly in absolute terms.

Automakers are now accelerating EV plans because the policy direction is becoming clearer, and analysts expect EV market share to reach around 10% by 2028 and reach about 19% by 2032. That could push annual EV sales to roughly 500K in a few years and eventually to 1 million, making electric offerings harder to ignore for manufacturers.

Currently, the EV market in India is still dominated by Tata Motors, Mahindra (MAHMF), and JSW MG Motor, which together accounted for nearly 85% of March EV sales, but new entrants and partnerships are lining up as well, including Honda Motor's (HMC) first India EV tests and JSW Group's talks with Chinese automaker Chery. Meanwhile, Tesla (TSLA) formally entered India in July 2025 with its first showroom in Mumbai and the Model Y, but it launched at a premium price because the cars are imported and face steep duties. The Austin-based company remains a niche premium player rather than a mass-market force in India.

data.title: Ulta set to capitalize on bullish makeup cycle -- Jefferies
data.description:
“The importance of makeup cannot be overstated,” said Jefferies analyst Sydney Wagner, “and remains the highest-velocity category in beauty and 38% of ULTA sales.” With Ulta Beauty (ULTA) better positioned to capitalize on renewed makeup engagement and with a more favorable risk/reward, Wagner upgrades the stock to Buy from Hold and lifts the target price for the stock by 26% to $700.  

What differentiates the current beauty environment from previous, short-lived makeup trends is the growing alignment between consumer interest and supply-side response.  This encourages brands to increase innovation while retailers expand resets, assortments, and “shelf emphasis.”

Wagner believes this combination materially increases the likelihood that early demand will translate into sustained momentum, consistent with the early stages of a true category cycle rather than “episodic spikes in engagement.” 

Accordingly, Ulta Beauty (ULTA) recently changed its merchandise assortment, shifting from “catch-up” and from brands largely available on Amazon (AMZN) to offensive with more relevant brands and trend-led launches. And since this change, Ulta (ULTA) has improved the pace and relevance of new brand additions, moving beyond “gap-filling” to proactive.

“Ulta’s position as the industry’s most comprehensive, cross-price beauty destination is becoming more relevant as momentum builds across sub-categories,” Wagner writes, adding that as the makeup cycle gains traction, Ulta (ULTA) is better positioned to convert category momentum into “sustained shares and earnings durability.”

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors are also bullish on Ulta (ULTA) with an average Buy rating for the stock. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, however, is more cautious with a score of 2.99 out of 5, translating into a Hold rating based on low scores for its growth and valuation metrics.

Ulta Beauty (ULTA) shares are set up to trade in the green for a sixth consecutive day.

data.title: VNET Group CFO to step down
data.description:
• VNET Group (VNET) chief financial officer Qiyu Wang will resign effective April 30, 2026.
  • Peter Zhihua Zhang, appointed SVP of Operational Finance in February 2026, will continue overseeing financial operations.
